description:
## Parent

Parent epic: `bsm-dbh` — Filtering and search issue list views.

## Background

The Issue Detail pane must never show an Issue that is absent from the visible Issue List. Once view switching and search can hide rows, Beadsmith needs deterministic selection behavior. It should keep a selected Issue visible when the Issue remains in the current result set, clear it when hidden, and avoid surprising auto-restore later.

View switching also needs to reset the Issue List scroll position so a newly selected Issue List View opens at the top rather than inheriting an unrelated scroll offset.

## What to build

Coordinate selected Issue Detail state with active view/search visibility:

- If the currently selected Issue remains visible after a view or search change, keep showing its Issue Detail.
- If the currently selected Issue is hidden by a view or search change, clear the selection and show the empty Issue Detail state.
- Once cleared because it was hidden, do not automatically restore the selection if a later view/search change makes the Issue visible again.
- Switching Issue List Views resets the Issue List scroll position to the top.

## Technical Approach

Track selected Issue ID independently from visible rows, but reconcile it after active view/search changes. Use a visible-ID membership check rather than guessing from status or view type. Clearing should set selected Issue ID to null; it should not store a hidden previous selection for later restoration. When a selected ID remains visible through a different command-backed collection, render detail from the active visible collection or an explicitly normalized lookup that represents the visible Issue, not from a stale All-only array by accident.

Attach a ref to the Issue List scroll container or otherwise use an implementation that resets scroll only when the active Issue List View changes. Search changes are not required to reset scroll unless the implementation naturally does so, but view changes must.

## Considerations

- Selecting an Issue that appears through multiple views should remain selected when switching among views where it is still visible. If duplicate normalized records with the same ID are theoretically present across All/Ready/Blocked, prefer behavior that keeps the visible row and detail consistent rather than exposing mismatched fields.
- Search narrowing unrelated rows should not disrupt the user's current detail reading if the selected Issue still matches.
- Clearing selection should be observable in row `aria-current`/selected state and in the Issue Detail empty state.
- Avoid auto-selecting the first visible row; the existing no-auto-selection behavior should remain.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Selected Issue Detail remains populated when the selected Issue remains visible after a view/search change.
- [ ] Selected Issue Detail clears when the selected Issue is hidden by the active view or search query.
- [ ] Cleared selection does not auto-restore when the Issue later becomes visible again.
- [ ] Switching Issue List Views resets the Issue List scroll position to the top.
- [ ] The Issue Explorer still does not auto-select the first row after load or after filtering.
- [ ] When selection remains visible across a view switch, the detail pane stays consistent with the active visible Issue collection.

## Test Plan

- Component tests for keeping selection across view/search changes where the selected Issue remains visible.
- Component tests for clearing selection when view changes hide the selected Issue.
- Component tests for clearing selection when search hides the selected Issue.
- Component tests proving cleared selection does not auto-restore after clearing the query or switching back.
- Component or DOM tests proving view switching resets the scroll container.
title: Coordinate Issue Detail selection and list scroll with view/search changes
